questions.text: Keeping in view the size of atoms,which order is correct one?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Magnesium is a smaller atom with a smaller radius than strontium, this means the electron has a greater attraction to the nucleus. Also as strontium has more orbitals the electron has more shielding from the nucleus from the lower orbitals.

Since barium is placed lower than magnesium i.e., at the bottom in the second group of periodic table, therefore barium atom are larger in size than magnesium atom and hence barium atoms loses its valence electron more readily than magnesium atom and forms positive ions i.e., cation.

On moving from left to right in a periodic table, the atomic radius of an atom decreases due to the addition of an extra proton which leads to a stronger pull of electrons while the extra electron goes to the same principal energy level.While going down in a group, an extra energy level adds to the structure which leads to an increase in atomic radius. Thus, on moving down the group, the atomic radius increases.Cerium belongs to Lanthanoids, Tinbelongs to Group 16 and period 4, Ytterbium and Lutetiumalso belong to lanthanoids.Lanthanoids are considered in the sixth period.There is a sharp decrease in the atomic size of lanthanoids from as a result of lanthanoid contraction. The size of and is smaller than Sn.
Chlorine is a smaller atom than iodine so its outer electrons are closer to the nucleus.

questions.text: Which ion will have maximum value of heat of hydration?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Higher the charge, greater the hydration energy. On the other hand, smaller the size, greater the hydration energy. Charge is considered first comparison. Hence, Mg2+ has higher hydration energy than Na+

Therefore, lithium ion ($L{{i}^{+}}$) has the highest polarizing power. This polarizing power of the lithium is explained by the size and charge of the ion, as we know that the size of the lithium in the alkali metal ions is the smallest, due to which the ratio of charge and size is very high.
Hydration energy increases with an increase in the charge density. So ba2+ has the highest hydration enthalpy.
Hydration energy increases with an increase in the charge density. So Mg has the highest hydration enthalpy.

questions.text: Which element among the following belongs to group IVA of periodic table?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Barium is a metallic element, soft, and when pure is silvery white; it belongs to the alkaline earth group, chemically resembling calcium. The metal oxidizes very easily and should be kept under petroleum or other suitable oxygen-free liquids to exclude air. It is decomposed by water or alcohol.
iodine (I), chemical element, a member of the halogen elements, or Group 17 (Group VIIa) of the periodic table.

Tin is a chemical element found in Group 14 (or IVa) of the periodic table (i.e., the carbon family). The tin element symbol is Sn, which is derived from the Latin name for the element - stannum. It is a silvery-white metal that is resistant to corrosion. The atomic number of tin is 50 and atomic mass is 118.71.

The oxygen family, also called the chalcogens, consists of the elements found in Group 16 of the periodic table and is considered among the main group elements. It consists of the elements oxygen, sulfur, selenium, tellurium and polonium. These can be found in nature in both free and combined states.

questions.explanation: Tin is a chemical element found in Group 14 (or IVa) of the periodic table (i.e., the carbon family). The tin element symbol is Sn, which is derived from the Latin name for the element - stannum. It is a silvery-white metal that is resistant to corrosion. The atomic number of tin is 50 and atomic mass is 118.71.

questions.text: Which of the following is a typical transition metal?
questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.explanation: Group IIB elements (Zn, Cd and Hg) do not have a partially filled d-subshell either as elements or in any of their ionic state. Neither do they show typical properties of transition elements to any appreciable extent (except complex formation). Similarly, IIIB (Sc, Y and La) is another group of elements which do not show many of the properties associated with typical transition elements.

questions.text: The presence of double bond in a compound is sign of:

questions.text: Which compound shows maximum hydrogen bonding with water?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: Hydrogen bonding is defined as the formation of hydrogen bonds, which are a special type of attractive intermolecular forces that arise due to the dipole-dipole interaction between a hydrogen atom that is bonded to a highly electronegative atom and another highly electronegative atom that lies next to the hydrogen atom.In ethanol, Hydrogen is bound to more electronegative oxygen than triethylamine, where Hydrogen is bound to less electronegative nitrogen,Hence, Hydrogen bonding is maximum in ethanol.

questions.text: Ketones are prepared by oxidation of:
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The oxidation of alcohols is an important reaction in organic chemistry. Primary alcohols can be oxidized to form aldehydes and carboxylic acids; secondary alcohols can be oxidized to give ketones. Tertiary alcohols, in contrast, cannot be oxidized without breaking the molecule's C–C bonds.

The oxidation of alcohols is an important reaction in organic chemistry. Primary alcohols can be oxidized to form aldehydes and carboxylic acids; secondary alcohols can be oxidized to give ketones. Tertiary alcohols, in contrast, cannot be oxidized without breaking the molecule's C–C bonds.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a fatty acid?
questions.subject: Chemistry

options.explanation: The term “acetic” comes from “acetum,” the Latin word for vinegar. Acetic acid is a short-chain fatty acid that dissolves into acetate and hydrogen in your body.
